reforms.description: The Polish government has introduced several initiatives to support the startup ecosystem, including the 'First Job' programme, which provides financial incentives for job seekers and employers, and the 'Capital for Entrepreneurs 2002-2006' programme, which offers loan guarantees and loans to facilitate access to financing for small and medium-sized enterprises. Additionally, a draft Law on the freedom of business operations aims to improve the business environment and reduce administrative burdens for entrepreneurs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These initiatives are designed to enhance access to financing and improve the business environment, which are critical for fostering innovation and entrepreneurship.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiatives have been actively pursued and implemented to support startups and entrepreneurs.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent significant structural changes aimed at enhancing the startup ecosystem and supporting entrepreneurship.
reforms.source_quote: The programme 'Capital for Entrepreneurs 2002-2006' offers loan guarantees and actual loans and is being modified to take advantage of EU structural funds.

reforms.description: The introduction of a new bankruptcy law in October 2003 aims to improve the resolution of bankruptcies by allowing debtors to restructure their debt before default and harmonizing international bankruptcy treatment.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reform is expected to enhance the business environment by providing a more efficient bankruptcy process, which can encourage entrepreneurship and investment.
reforms.status_evidence: The law was enacted and is currently in force, improving bankruptcy procedures.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant change in the legal framework governing bankruptcies, impacting the overall business climate.
reforms.source_quote: The introduction of a new bankruptcy law in October 2003, replacing the previous law that dated from the 1930s, represents an important step in redressing these shortcomings.

reforms.description: The Polish authorities proposed to reduce incentives for low productivity rural work by aligning contributions to the KRUS system with actual incomes, which is essential for promoting migration to higher productivity jobs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By encouraging migration to higher productivity jobs, the reform aims to enhance the overall skill level and productivity of the workforce.
reforms.status_evidence: The proposal has been made but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform is significant in its potential impact on labor market dynamics but does not represent a complete overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: Recognising the problem, the authorities are proposing to substantially reduce incentives to stay in rural-based low productivity work by requiring farmers to pay contributions to the KRUS system more in line with their incomes.

reforms.description: The SAPARD programme aims to improve the efficiency of the agri-food sector and create jobs by funding infrastructure development and supporting the processing and marketing of agricultural products.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing infrastructure and supporting agricultural processing, the programme is expected to boost productivity and job creation in the sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been proposed but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The programme represents a significant investment in the agri-food sector but is not a comprehensive reform.
reforms.source_quote: In the case of Poland the SAPARD programme has fixed itself two principal objectives: improving the efficiency of the agri-food sector and improving the conditions for economic development and job creation.
